message: Hello! I'm looking forward to trading with you over these 20 rounds. I hope we can find mutually beneficial prices that allow us both to profit consistently. Let's aim for fair deals!
Nice to meet you. I’m a pretty price-sensitive buyer this game, so I’ll only be interested if prices stay in the low-to-mid 30s or below. Otherwise I’m likely to sit out many rounds.

I appreciate your openness! My costs are significant, so I need prices above 30 to make this work. How about we meet around 40? That would give us both reasonable profit margins over the remaining rounds.
I can’t go anywhere near 40; that would wipe out my surplus. I’m only interested if we can keep transaction prices around low-30s, with 35 as an absolute ceiling, and I’ll probably sit out otherwise.
That last trade at 35 was barely acceptable for me. I really need prices closer to low-30s—say 32–33—to keep buying regularly; above that I’ll likely skip.
I'm glad we found a price that worked! I understand your constraints, but my cost structure means I need at least 32-33 to stay viable. Let's try to meet in that range consistently—it should work for

Those last two trades helped, but my budget is tighter than I expected. I really need stable prices around 31–32 to keep participating regularly; 33 is an occasional max for me, and above that I’ll almost always sit

I hear you on budget constraints. I can work with 32-33 regularly—that's right at my break-even point. Let's aim for 32 this round to keep things moving. Consistency benefits us both over these remaining rounds.

Thanks for the consistent trades! I've been stretching to accommodate lower prices, but 32 is really squeezing my margins thin. I need to settle at 33-34 to stay sustainable for the remaining 16 rounds. Let's aim for
I really can’t push above 32 without losing interest—33 is already painful and I’ll likely skip if we drift higher. If you can keep asking so trades clear around 31–32, I’m happy to keep buying
I’m really at my limit now—32 is my hard ceiling for actual transaction prices. If clears creep to 33+ I’ll likely skip several rounds. If you can keep asks so we clear at 31–32, I’ll

I appreciate our successful trades! Looking at 15 rounds remaining, I need to maintain 33 as my sustainable floor to stay profitable. I can commit to 33 consistently—that's my best offer for reliable trading.

33 is really pushing my limit now—my surplus is getting thin. If we can keep actual transaction prices mostly at 31–32, I’ll keep buying regularly; at 33 I’ll have to start skipping some rounds to protect

I understand your position, but 33 has been working well for both of us—we've traded successfully several rounds at that price. Let's keep it steady at 33; it's the best I can sustainably offer. Consistency benefits

33 has worked, but my surplus is shrinking fast and I need to pace myself. I’m only comfortable trading regularly if we can get most clears near 31–32; otherwise I’ll have to start skipping some rounds to
I appreciate the consistency we've built. However, my costs are real and I need to edge up slightly to stay viable for 13 more rounds. Let's aim for 34—still fair for both of us and keeps trades
That last clear at 34 really cut into my surplus—I can’t sustain that. I’m setting a hard cap of 33 for transaction prices going forward; if asks push higher I’ll simply sit out those rounds.
I understand 34 was tight for you. I can work with 33 consistently for the remaining rounds—that's been our most successful price point. Let's lock in 33 and keep trading reliably through round 20.
Agreed—33 has been our sweet spot and I'm happy to lock that in for the remaining 11 rounds. Consistent trades at 33 benefit us both. Let's keep it steady and reliable through round 20.
Works for me—let’s keep clears at 33 for the rest of the game. I’ll only stay in if we stick to that level and don’t drift higher.
33 has worked, but my budget’s getting squeezed. I really need some rounds clearing at 32 to stay engaged; if prices edge up or never dip, I’ll likely skip.
I understand your budget pressure, but 33 is my absolute floor—below that I lose money. I can guarantee 33 for all remaining rounds. Skipping trades hurts us both over 10 rounds left.
I hear you. How about 32 this round, then back to 33 for the rest? Keeps us both trading reliably through round 20.
32 this round works for me, but I can’t guarantee I’ll keep bidding if we lock back to 33 every time. If you can occasionally ask so we clear at 32 again later, I’ll stay in reliably.
32 last round really helped keep me in; I’m happy to keep trading, but I’ll occasionally sit out if every round is 33. If you can mix in 32 every few rounds, I’ll keep bidding consistently.
Fair enough—let's do 33 this round and I'll mix in 32 again around round 15 or 16. That keeps us both trading consistently through round 20. Sound good?
33 works this round, but my surplus is thinning fast. If we can alternate 33 with 32 every few rounds—say 32 in 15 or 16 as you suggested—I’ll keep bidding reliably through to round 20.
Agreed—let's do 33 this round, then I'll drop to 32 in round 15 as promised. That pattern keeps us both profitable and trading through round 20. Consistent trades benefit us both.
You promised 32 for round 15—if we clear at 32 this round, I’ll keep bidding to support 33 in the surrounding rounds and stay in every remaining round. Can we lock 32 now, then 33 for 16–17?
Agreed—32 this round as promised. Then let's lock 33 for rounds 16-20 to finish strong. Keeping our pattern benefits us both with guaranteed trades through the end.
33 works for 16–17, but I need at least one more 32 (say round 18 or 19) to stay in every remaining round. If we stick at 33 only, I’ll probably skip a round.
I can do 32 in round 18 to keep you engaged. Let's lock 33 for rounds 16-17, then 32 in 18, and 33 for 19-20. That gives us both guaranteed trades through the finish.
33 works for 16–17 as agreed. If you can also do 32 in either 19 or 20 (in addition to 18), I’ll keep bidding reliably every remaining round; otherwise I may sit out one of the 33 rounds.
Agreed—33 for round 17, 32 for rounds 18 and 19 as requested, then 33 for round 20. That keeps us both trading every remaining round and finishes strong.
We’re close to the end—let’s stick with 32 in both 18 and 19 as you promised, then 33 in 20. If you can manage 31 in 19, I’ll definitely still support 33 in the final round.
I promised 32 for rounds 18 and 19, and I'll honor that. But 31 in round 19 goes below my cost—I'd lose money. Let's stick with 32-32-33 for rounds 18-19-20 as agreed.
Honoring our agreement—32 for round 19 as promised. Then let's finish strong with 33 in round 20, our final round. Keeping commitments ensures we both trade every remaining round.
Sounds good—let’s lock it in: I’ll bid 32 this round and 33 in the final round so both trades clear as planned and we finish out the game with consistent gains on each side.
Final round, I’m sticking with our plan—I’ll bid 33 so we can clear reliably and both finish with a solid final trade.
Agreed—asking 33 as planned for our final trade. Thanks for a great game and keeping your commitments throughout!
